pattern: Sort ascending and pair adjacent elements: summing every other value starting from the smallest maximizes the total of the per-pair minimums.
hints: Each pair contributes only its smaller element, so you want to avoid wasting large values as the discarded maximum of a pair.
Sorting the array lets you pair neighbors so that each large value is sacrificed against the value just below it.
After sorting, the answer is the sum of the elements at even indices.

stress.description: random even-length array within value bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    pairs = rng.randint(1, max(1, size // 2))
    arr = [rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(2 * pairs)]
    return [arr]
stress.note: length is always even as required
